issues.title: Unused variable warnings
issues.detail: Six instances of `let body: Value = resp.json().await.unwrap_or_default()` where body is declared but never used. Should be prefixed with underscore (_body) to suppress warnings.

issues.title: Future incompatibility notices
issues.detail: Dependencies redis v0.24.0 and sqlx-postgres v0.7.4 contain code rejected by future Rust versions. Not immediately blocking but should be tracked for updates.

observations: Total tests: 40 (15 expected, 25 unexpected)

Tests ran across multiple projects (Mobile Safari)

issues.title: Lock API endpoint returns 400 instead of 200
issues.detail:
Multiple test cases expect POST /v1/lock to return status 200 but receive 400. This occurs at:
- Line 172: 'duplicate nonce prevents lock replay' test
- Line 234: 'multiple locks create distinct lock_ids and SR_0s' test

The API is rejecting valid lock requests. This may indicate:
1. Missing or incorrect request body validation
2. API implementation not yet accepting lock data in expected format
3. Backend validation logic too strict or incorrectly implemented

issues.title: Test retries exhausted without pass
issues.detail: Tests failed across all 3 retry attempts (retry 0, 1, 2), indicating consistent failure—not a flaky test. This is a systemic issue with the lock API endpoint.

issues.title: Partial test suite pass masks integration failure
issues.detail: While validation tests (expired lock, missing fields) correctly pass, the core positive-path lock creation tests fail. This suggests the test suite may have inconsistent coverage or the API partially implements the lock interface.

issues.title: RPC infrastructure timeout
issues.detail: The Sepolia RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org) failed to respond within timeout window. Cloudflare reports the origin host at rpc.sepolia.org did not complete the request (HTTP 522). This is a transient infrastructure issue, not a contract or code regression.
